summary: The National Research Council of Canada will invest over $900 million through Canada’s Defence Industrial Strategy to accelerate the development and commercialization of quantum technologies for national defence. Announced by Minister David J. McGuinty at the University of Waterloo, the funding targets quantum computing, communications, and sensors, aiming to enhance military capabilities and reinforce the domestic defence sector. The Canadian defence industry already supports over 81,000 jobs and contributes nearly $10 billion to GDP. These investments are part of a broader goal to reach 2 percent of gross domestic product spent on defence by 2025–2026, aligning Canada with the latest North Atlantic Treaty Organization pledge. The initiative is the largest collaboration between the National Research Council and the Department of National Defence to date.
